son. Will the Players challenge the Team Rocket grunt with

their Pokťmon or jump on them? Will they return the Pokť-

mon to its rightful trainer or become villains themselves? A

GM will continue to narrate as more and more decisions

are made by Players as the story unfolds.

The game is played in a world where many fantastic ani-

mals are able to be energized and stored nicely in a ma-

chine called a Pokť Ball. These animals are called "pocket

monsters," or Pokťmon for short, and they are carried as        What are the rules for?
tools, friends and partners by Pokťmon Trainers. Trainers

have various skills and abilities each defined by a Trainer's   Basically, you as a player, can direct your Trainer to try

goal. There are Ace Trainers, who focus all of their energy and do anything you want. However, most of your actions

into making their Pokťmon the best fighters they can.           are limited to what a real human might be capable of. In

Breeders, who raise Pokťmon as if they are their own chil- that sense, most rules are actually for breaking common

dren and groom them up to be powerful. The Researcher sense and making you exceptional in the exceptional world

they confront a creature they've never met. Rangers are         rules for how to use your Pokťmon to fly. While you might

their feats. The Coordinator employs their Pokťmon in           Pokťdex books as if you have memorized it. While you

Contests and battle as she shows off their beauty.              might not be able to look at a person's face and discern if

   The game has no definite goal and therefore has no ab-       they are lying to you, an Insight check's rules may help you
solute end. A GM can have an overarching plot that in-          know whether or not an NPC is being honest with you.

volves visiting tens of cities that would take over 100 hours

of gameplay to experience. As a result, players may find

themselves getting together weekly or monthly and contin-

uing play from where a previous session left off. As players

overcome obstacles they will grow, as will their Pokťmon,

and together will become stronger and stronger as they

continue to face greater and greater threats to defeat. With

no absolute goal, the game continues indefinitely≠ but the

story for one Trainer may end. Perhaps they retire, or meet

a mournful end? Either way, the game can continue if the

Player creates a new Trainer to continue with. As long as

the Players and GM are having fun creating a story together                                                                     Welcome

Pokťmon: Tabletop Adventures never has to end.

                       First: Stats            Page 6-11                               of your Pokťmon will have individual needs and keeping
                                                                                       too many may become a burden for you, or your Pokťmon

                          Stats are designated as Hit Points, Attack, Defense, Spe- friends.

                       cial Attack, Special Defense, and Speed. Though you might

                       create your stats after you've learned more about which

                       Class you'll want to play as, you'll need to know how stats     The following sections will bring you through this trainer

                       work in order to understand how many of each Class's fea- creation process. Let's get into it!

                       tures work. You'll probably finalize this after picking which

                       class you want because in order to excel in a Class you will

                       want your Stats to be relevant to that Class. For example,

                       the Ranger who desires physical dexterity will want a lot of

                       Speed, and not have their Attack stat be their highest stat.

                       Second: Class           Page 12-89

                          You'll be picking a Trainer Class. Generally, a Class is a
                       Trainer's specialty or job. While any Trainer may hatch a
                       Pokťmon egg to care for and raise, a Breeder would spe-
                       cialize in doing so. Likewise, any Trainer may battle with
                       their Pokťmon, but an Ace Trainer specializes in empower-
                       ing their Pokťmon during battle. Based on your class, spe-
                       cific stats will be more desirable and further specializations
                       will be made available as you become more capable. For
                       example, the Researcher can further narrow their specialty
                       to become a Photographer or Scientist. You will gain these
                       Advanced Classes by leveling up. Usually, you will start your
                       character at level 1 but different GMs might want more ex-
                       citing starts to a campaign and ask you to build a level 3, 5,
                       or even level 10 Trainer.

                       Third: Origins          Page 90-99

                       ble that you already had a character and background for         - If a rule breaks another rule, the rule-breaking rule has

                       assigned their Stats. That's perfectly okay! The Origins are
                       just some additional bits that help to flesh out some me-       - Numbers are hard. If you ever need to divide, round
                       chanical aspects while also giving some additional areas of            down.
                       expertise for a Player's Trainer.

Attack Skills:                                                Special Attack Skills:

The attack skill, athletics, basically covers everything that Special Attack skills have to do with your intelligence, most-

will help you during strength related skill checks.           ly. The skills here are pretty diverse and have to do with

Pokťmon Handling

   When it comes to Pokťmon, there's just so much to
know. Handling specifically has to do with approaching
and interacting with Pokťmon who are strangers to you or
Pokťmon who really do not like you. You could use Pokť-
mon handling to slowly approach a wary wild or to attempt
to calm a rampaging Tauros. Pokťmon Handling might
even be checked when you're mounted on a Pokťmon who
isn't very comfortable with you yet.

vate and kept track of by GMs. Loyalty is tricky, because      ment that will define the party's future actions in the cam-
                                                               paign, or define the trainers themselves, they can gain a
you need to spend a great deal of time roleplaying the rela-
tionship you have with your Pokťmon in order for the GM        point of honor.

to show the Pokťmon's behaviors towards you. Starter

Pokťmon, or pets that you've brought along on your jour-

ney might have an edge on getting to loyalty 5, but while a

Pokťmon may be loyal and love your trainer, a Pokťmon

could still only be at loyalty 4 in the GM's notebooks. After

some important breakthrough or moment of courage be-

tween your trainer and one of their Pokťmon, and after

hours of game play with that trainer and Pokťmon, a GM

might award your trainer with an honor. Where loyalty is

explained later in the Player's Handbook, you can see what

other benefits your trainer and Pokťmon gain once having

a loyalty level of 5.

    Sometimes a trainer in your party will receive an honor when you do not. While that may feel bad, have faith
that you will find an opportunity for you to catch up. Trainers are always helping each other in many Pokťmon me-
dia, so maybe you could help to prepare an ally for their next Gym challenge or help them to train for a contest
and they could do the same for you.

**During these levels, you get +1 to two stats, +1d4 max HP, and a new class                                                 Leveling Up

Additional levels require 5 more honors each time, and continue to progress all of your classes at once.

If you took a new Base Class at level 3, 7, or 11, you will not be able to gain its level 15 feature- you will only
gain the level 15 feature from your first class.

Ace Trainer - Ace Trainers focus on the battle prowess of their Pokťmon, always seeking new strategies and combat tricks.

Primary stats: Attack, Sp. Attack    Advanced Classes: Stat Ace, Strategist, Tag Battler, Type Ace, Underdog

Breeder - A Breeder lovingly raises Pokťmon as if they are their own children from the first day they hatch.

Primary stats: Defense, Sp. Defense  Advanced Classes: Botanist, Chef, Evolver, Medic, Move Tutor

Coordinator - Always at the center of attention, Coordinators invent beautiful strategies to dazzle and amaze.

Primary stats: Sp. Defense, Speed    Advanced Classes: Choreographer, Coach, Designer, Groomer, Rising Star

Ranger - Protecting the Pokťmon world is the primary goal of a Ranger and they do so by working with wild Pokťmon.

Primary stats: Defense, Speed        Advanced Classes: Invoker, Officer, Rider, Special Operative, Survivalist

Researcher - Researchers analyze data at a rate rivaling a Pokťdex and use it to find solutions to any problem.

Primary stats: Sp. Attack, Sp. Defense Advanced Classes: Archeologist, Ball Smith, Photographer, Scientist, Watcher

Ace Trainer  ACE TRAINER

             Strength, Focus, Ingenuity

                The Ace Trainer is an expert battler, using wit and
             strength to maneuver the battlefield and power through
             any forces that might stand in their way with their Pokť-
             mon. Many Ace Trainers love the league circuit and do eve-
             rything in their power to join championships and fight for
             first place. When further developing their battle prowess,
             Ace Trainers delve into specific expertise having to do with
             various specialties ranging from Pokťmon types, to Pokť-
             mon technical stratagem, or even the stage of development
             a Pokťmon might be.

             Favored Stats: Attack and Special Attack

             Advanced Class Options: Stat Ace, Strategist, Tag Battler,
             Type Ace, Underdog

You still need to make an Accuracy Check.

Lock-On - Ranged(40ft) Normal Effect: 1/day. Lock-On can't
miss. Your next attack against the same target will not
miss.

Quick Attack - Melee Normal Attack: At-Will 2d6. Quick At-
tack has Priority.

Breeder  BREEDER

         Guidance, Family, Love

            Raising a Pokťmon from birth and imbuing them with
         your care and nurturing is powerfully rewarding. Many
         Breeders spend hours caring for eggs and then the Pokť-
         mon who hatches from it. They teach it everything it needs
         to know and more in ways that other trainers might fall
         short while encouraging its growth and maturity. Breeders
         who take more care into their work might find themselves
         inventing things to teach their Pokťmon, taking a special
         interest in their medical care, or even by revealing extreme-
         ly powerful bonds through new evolutions.

         Favored Stats: Defense and Special Defense

         Advanced Class Options: Botanist, Chef, Evolver, Medic,
         Move Tutor

         Skill Talents: Choose two; Constitution, Diplomacy/
         Persuasion, History, Medicine, Nature, Pokťmon Handling

Miniature Specimen: You apply a theory towards creating
smaller, but still just as effective berry plants. You can grow
two plants in a portable planter at the same time, and the
berries these plants produce are small enough to fit in the
palm of your hand. Berries produced with the miniature
specimen feature cannot be stolen from any Pokťmon who
is holding onto one during battle.

Level 11

Do No Harm: Your fervor in battle and willingness to risk
your life while protecting others burns brightly as you save
lives around you every day. On any turn where you haven't
attacked any targets and have not issued any attack com-
mands to any of your Pokťmon, your Treat Minor Wound,
Treat Wound, Treat Major Wound and Treat Serious
Wound features heal an additional 10 hit points, but grant
10 less temporary hit points. You may not issue attack
commands to any Pokťmon after benefitting from the Do
No Harm feature.

Level 10

Class Time: Putting in the time to tutor Pokťmon can be
consuming for them, but you can split your attention with-
out problems. While tutoring Pokťmon, you can work with

different Move Tutor features, or teach the same thing to

Coordinator  COORDINATOR

             Performer, Daring, Star

                Center stage is the best place to be for a Coordinator.
             Whether its at a contest or on the field of battle, they ma-
             nipulate the audience and their opponents to pay attention
             whether they want to or not≠ but they really, really do. Co-
             ordinators have a flair for showing off the very best of eve-
             ryone and when they grow, they become fashionistas,
             award-winning celebrities.

             Favored Stats: Special Defense and Speed

             Advanced Class Options: Choreographer, Coach, Designer,
             Groomer, Rising Star

             Skill Talents: Choose two; Acrobatics, Athletics, Bluff/
             Deception, Diplomacy/Persuasion, Perform, Sleight of Hand

overwhelms you and you know exactly how to impress
those in front of you. 1/day, when a contest starts, you are
secretly informed of one Star Point category that you do
not know. Once you are level 11, you are secretly informed
of two Star Point categories. Additionally, whenever you
are rolling a Perception check you may add your Special
Defense or Speed modifier to your result.

ways.

   A Choreographer teaches Pokťmon to always move with

a way around an incoming attack from enemies.

Ranger  RANGER

        Protector, Servant, Leader

           As the guardians of the Pokťmon world's natural spaces,
        Rangers serve and protect Pokťmon, putting themselves
        on the line. Rangers are skilled acrobats, navigating all
        sorts of terrain in order to rescue anyone or any Pokťmon
        in need and use their focus and trusty styler to harness the
        help of wild Pokťmon, enabling a ranger to solve any prob-
        lem and overcome any obstacle even when outnumbered.

        Favored Stats: Defense and Speed

        Advanced Class Options: Invoker, Officer, Rider,
        Special Operative, Survivalist

        Skill Talents: Choose two; Acrobatics, Athletics, Diplomacy/
        Persuasion, Perception, Pokťmon Handling, Stealth

        Special: You must have the Ranger Union/Law Enforcement origin in order
        to take the Ranger base class.

        Steel-type. This weather disappears after 2 mins.

        weather, Fire-type attacks deal an additional 8 damage and

        pears after 2 mins.

OFFICER                                                                                                                     Ranger

Gumshoe rangers on the beat.

   Officers are often more concerned with the city limits
and making sure that people are just as safe from the wilds
as they are from each other. Paying close attention to the
manmade world around them gives them leads, pressing
suspicious individuals leads to shadowy organizations, and
pursuing threads of conspiracies can help to take down
major threats to the Pokťmon world.

RIDER                                                                                                                                         Ranger

Acrobatic prowess on mounts keeps you right in the action.

   Riders have mastered their balance and handling to stay
on top of battle while also managing other trainer's rides
and keeping their team in a uniform herd through the bat-
tlefield. They have a natural way about finding the best
ways to stay on mounts and keep engaged in the action
while impressing everyone with their style.

Researcher  PHOTOGRAPHER

            An artistic eye for study and observation.

               A skilled eye and a steady hand makes for an excellent
            photographer. While the camera itself could be basic, your
            expertise brings out the best of any shot. This kind of re-
            search makes for an alternative to strictly academic stud-
            ies. Who knows? With enough practice you could enter pic-
            tures into art galleries or even make a living selling your
            goods.

WATCHER                                                                                                                       Researcher

Talent with a pencil, patience for the sketch.

   Watchers are field researchers who go out and study,
track, and locate wild Pokťmon. They have a natural calm
about them and use their passive presence to take in infor-
mation and replicate it with their artistic ability. Watchers
watch everything and take in information that would other-
wise go unnoticed by most.

Origin s  Origins                                                             Lifestyle indicates what kind of comfort level you're used
                                                                           to, and also indicates what kind of starting credits you'll

             Each trainer had various life experiences leading up to       have. You could forego a `better' quality of life to have a
          the moment where they decided to be a Pokťmon Trainer.           little more credits saved up for the adventure ahead. Work
          Some may have found the league battles online or on tele-        that out with your GM if you're going to change your origin
          vision thrilling and decided the same thrill and fame that       slightly.

          competitors sought is for them. Others may have found            Lifestyle:   Savings:

          themselves desiring more adventure in their life after years     Difficult    5,000-15,000
          of managing a storefront without ever owning a Pokťmon           Modest       20,000-40,000
          themselves. Some might have spent most of their life with        Comfortable  45,000-75,000
          a family in front of an office desk and decided that they        Wealthy      1,000,000
          wanted more. Maybe you've been a trainer for a while, in

          the safety of a Gym as a mentee or within a dangerous or-        Difficult: People with difficult lifestyles usually live in

          ganization doing various tasks for the boss?                     small apartments or old homes and rarely enjoy a meal out

             Regardless of their beginnings, each trainer now has          with friends and family. While they don't usually have a
          some stats and a base class. These origins will help flesh       hard time keeping themselves fed, they might not have
          out your character's backgrounds and give them more skill        consistent sleep schedules, may move often, or might not
          talents, a feature, some starting equipment, and the             hold consistent work hours. This lifestyle is rarely a choice,
          chance to further expand on who each of them is.                 but usually a byproduct of the background that a trainer
                                                                           might have in life.
             It's possible that your character spent years at a college,

          went to medical school to become a doctor, then became

          an explorer, traveling the world. While this means that

          technically, they have origins as an academic, a doctor, and

          a backpacker, you would not claim all three as your origin.

          Instead, imagine which phase of your character's life

          helped to shape them into who they are? At which point of

          their life did they feel that they were most defined? This will

          help you choose your origins for your characters and from

          there, you can gain the benefits of those backgrounds.

              Origins             Lifestyle                                   Modest: Modest lifestyles are nice, with nice food availa-
                                                                           ble once or twice a week, and a space that fits your needs
              Academic            Difficult                                to live. You usually have consistent sleep and work sched-

Starting Equipment: Anything you want, short of rare or

Feature: Big Bucks

   You're rich, don't sweat the small stuff. When purchas-
ing goods, as long as you are not spending more than
three thousand Pokťcredits a day, you do not deplete your
savings. If you plan to purchase something unconvention-
al, like a building or rare shiny Pokťmon, consider your
origin and whether or not you would deplete your money
for a period of time. For example, if you are expanding
your hotel chain and building a new property on a foreign
beach, you might not be able to do that again for a year or
so and you might be wary of spending more than a thou-
sand credits a day instead of the normal three thousand.

Family/Species Name

   At the top of each page in the Pokťdex, each Pokťmon is
listed as a part of a Species grouping. Individual stages of a
single Pokťmon's evolutionary line are not part of the same
Species, but are part of the Species' family. When talking
about a specific evolutionary line, or branched evolutionary
tree, it is common to refer to the line or branch by the Spe-
cies name of the first Stage of the line, but this is not always
true. For example, to talk about the Pichu, Pikachu, and
Raichu evolutionary line, you could call it the Pichu or the
Pikachu evolutionary family. To refer to the Eevee, Jolteon,
Vaporeon, Flareon, Umbreon, Espeon, Glaceon and
Leafeon evolutionary branch you could call it the Eevee
evolutionary line.

                                                                 Pokťmon Passives affect how they work in battle. They
                                                              are static abilities that constantly affect how their offensive
                                                              moves work, or sometimes triggered consequences of a
                                                              foe attacking them. There are two types of passives. Stat
                                                              passives and ability passives that are diverse and grouped
                                                              into a long list in the Pokťdex books and after this section
                                                              of the Player's Handbook.

                                                                        Forest:
         The diet entry explains what kind of food the Pokťmon
                                                                        Most trainers are familiar with temperate, broadleaf
         needs to survive. Carnivores primarily feed on other Pokť-
         mon, while herbivores will usually need leafy food, vegeta- forests. With mild, but seasonal weather and healthy tree
         bles, nuts and fruits to survive. Omnivores can eat plants or growth, they play home to many common Pokťmon. Many

         their own food and energy. Some of the stranger diets
         some Pokťmon have are that of a terravore; one who feeds would do well starting their adventures in such areas.

         on the minerals found in rock and dirt around them, or the

         nullivore; which does not need to eat anything to sustain

         itself for various reasons. There's also saprophytes feed on

         decaying matter or minerals off the ground, ergovores feed

         on electricity and other energy, glacievores survive by con-

         suming ice and water, pollutivores feed on pollution of any

         kind, even psiotrophs who sustain themselves off the

         thoughts and life force of others.

Grasslands:                                                    magma pools inside of them, only Pokťmon that can with-

Grasslands covers a pretty wide range of habitats              Fire-types thrive in these areas, though sturdier Rock-types

and while similar, they are specifically varied and so you'll and the occasional Poison-type can be found here as well.

often see these entries with additional information as they

cover woodlands, fields, meadows, savannas, plains, and

prairies. Grasslands, plains, prairies, and savannas are

characterized by a lack of tall trees and an abundance of

tall grass. They are warm with seasonal rainfall; dry sea-

sons are exceptionally devoid of water. Brushfires are an

occasional occurrence in these areas, a phenomenon exac-

erbated by Fire- and Electric-types that can start them. Poi-

son- and Normal-types are also a regular sight in the tall

grass. Open fields and meadows are turfed with lush, short

grass and wildflowers. A near-total lack of trees grants

these areas with gentle breezes, open skies, and warm

sunlight. With temperate weather and moderate rainfall,

many Normal- and Flying-types can be found in these habi-

tats. Oddly, Electric-types often dwell here as well, perhaps

so they can be unobstructed by trees during lightning

storms. Woodlands are a bit less dense than forests, allow-

ing them to support more shrubs and bushes. They often

form in areas where a few trees have fallen, allowing flow-

ers and tall grasses to take advantage of open light. Damp,

rotting logs and plentiful shade from remaining trees make

woodlands ideal for Bug-types; when night falls, Dark-

types, Ghost-types, and Fairy-types may also appear in

these whimsical habitats.

the skill as well. If a Pokťmon starts with a move that ena-

                Freezer - When a Pokťmon has the freezer skill, they can
                produce ice at a controlled rate. They can make puffs of
                snow, or ice cubes, or spread icicles across surfaces. As
                their action, a Pokťmon with freezer can target an area on
                the ground and slowly turn it into icy, difficult terrain. They
                can make one 5x5 ft. area into icy, difficult terrain or fluffy,
                snowy, difficult terrain per 6 seconds. If a Pokťmon learns
                the move Aurora Beam, Blizzard, Freeze-Dry, Frost Breath,
                Ice Beam, Ice Fang, Ice Punch, Icy Wind, Powder Snow, or
                Sheer Cold, and does not have the freezer skill, they gain
                freezer. (can create ice)

                Gilled - A gilled Pokťmon can breathe underwater. It never
                needs to come up for air, remaining underwater for as long
                as it wants to. (can breathe underwater)

                                                                                   of these Pokťmon in the air, but they can't go higher than

Pokťmon Skills  Threaded - If a Pokťmon has the threaded skill they may
                move around with their threaded skill by shooting out silk
                webs or strong vines. A threaded movement is when a
                Pokťmon targets an object less than 30 ft. away and shoots
                a strong, thin line of silk, or extends a vine from them-
                selves, and then quickly retracts that silk, or vine, pulling
                itself towards the object if the Pokťmon is lighter than the
                object or pulling the object towards the Pokťmon if the
                object is lighter than it is. If you target another Pokťmon or
                person with a threaded movement, roll your accuracy
                check against their Speed stat. You may still use a move on
                the same turn you perform a threaded movement. If a
                Pokťmon learns the move Electro Web, Spider Web, Vine
                Whip or Power Whip, or the stat passive String Shot, and
                does not have the threaded skill, they gain threaded. (can
                move around on spun threads/vines)

                                                                                                                          113
                                                                                   Fossil Reanimation

                                                                                      Pokťmon can be caught, hatched, even traded for or
                                                                                   purchased but maybe the strangest way to acquire a new
                                                                                   Pokťmon is to find a fossil and bring it to a reanimation
                                                                                   laboratory. Often only one or two may exist across entire
                                                                                   regions, with maybe only a little more than one thousand in
                                                                                   all of the Pokťmon world. When you have a fossil from a
                                                                                   Pokťmon, whether the Pokťmon is extinct or not, you can
                                                                                   bring it to one of these labs to try and reanimate a Pokť-
                                                                                   mon.

                                                                                      Trainers with enough money to use their facilities, or
                                                                                   maybe just a foot in the door asking a friend inside for
                                                                                   help, lets you make a reanimation check.

                      Each species' eggs hatch as various rates, but they all         Reanimation checks take three hours, whether in a la-
                   need the same thing. Consistent warmth and gentle care.         boratory or an Archeologist is using their portable reanima-
                   Dropping an egg or leaving it out in a blizzard is devastating  tor. Roll 1d20 to see what happens to the fossil.

                   to its hatching time (except in corner cases where an egg Roll Result

                   contains an Ice-type Pokťmon where it may still be okay).       1    The Pokťmon is instantly reanimated, the fossil is
                   After dropping an egg, its possible it may even not hatch at         destroyed and the reanimation machine is de-
                                                                                        stroyed. The reanimated Pokťmon flees in the con-
                   all. There are carrying cases for the extra careful trainers         fusion. It will take a total of five hours to repair any
                                                                                        damage to the machine.
                   that are detailed in the items section of the Player's Hand-

                   book.

                      When you touch a Pokť Ball to your hatched Pokťmon, it       2-4  The fossil is destroyed and nothing is reanimated.
                   will be captured without fail.

                                                                                   5 - 8 The fossil is not destroyed but nothing is reani-

                                                                                        mated.

                                                                                        the fossil is still intact. On 1, 2, or 3 the fossil is

                                                                                        destroyed.

                                       Dealing Damage

Combat restructure and editing by Sol  Before rolling the damage of your attack, you need to

                                       determine its effectiveness against the target. The Pokť-

                                       mon world has eighteen different elemental types that in-

                                       teract with each other in different ways. For example, Wa-

                                       ter attacks are more effective against Fire-type Pokťmon.

                                       When determining an attack's effectiveness, you only ever

                                       look at the elemental type of the move and the target, not

                                       the attacker. This is because most Pokťmon are capable of

                                       using attacks that are not aligned with their own elemental

                                       type.                                                          If a move loses all its damage dice, or would have more

                                       Some Pokťmon are weak to certain types. Flying and             damage dice removed than it possesses (such as a move

                                       against the target(s), using whatever die is already being their Same-type Attack Bonus.

                                       and is super-effective, it'll deal 4d10 damage, instead of        Some attacks deal a specific amount of damage on hit.

                                                                Stunned

                                                                   A Stunned character can't take turns. While stunned, a
                                                                character loses its next turn. On the following turn, it is no
                                                                longer stunned.

        124
Pokťmon Passives                                                   In the Pokťdex, stat passives are already added to
                                                                each Pokťmon's stats.
   In addition to their skills that affect how a Pokťmon in-
teracts with the world, Pokťmon also have passives that         Special Attack Stat Passives
affect their stats, their attacks, or sometimes change them
into different forms in special situations. Passives are split  Metal Sound    (+1 Special Attack)
into two groups: Stat Passives and Ability Passives. Ability    Nasty Plot     (+1 Special Attack)
Passives have no real limitations on them, where Stat Pas-      Fake Tears     (+2 Special Attack)
sives have several rules.                                       Tail Glow      (+3 Special Attack)

   A Pokťmon cannot have more than three Stat Passives          Special Defense Stat Passives
at a time and they can't have more than one Stat Passive
from the same category. If a Pokťmon acquires a new Stat        Confide        (+1 Special Defense)
Passive that breaks one of these two restrictions, the new      Amnesia        (+2 Special Defense)
Stat Passive replaces another existing Stat Passive. When it    Captivate      (+2 Special Defense)
does, remember to undo the stat changes to the Pokťmon          Eerie Impulse  (+2 Special Defense)
before applying the new Stat Passive.

               Ranged                                                          targets other than yourself, including allies, so be careful

               are defined per attack. If attacking a target, you must have

               a direct line of sight in order to have a chance at hitting

               them. Blocking terrain will not allow you to attempt an at-

               tack, while hindering terrain will simply make it more diffi-

               cult to hit the target. For every bit of hindering terrain be-

               tween yourself and the target, your accuracy check will be -

               1 unless you are using a move with Blast, Burst, or Wave.

Pokťmon Moves     Depending on whether it is Melee or Ranged, a Beam,
               Burst, Blast, or Wave move affects which types of abilities it
               functions with. For example, a Melee(5 ft burst) move would
               not work with the passive Compound Eyes, but could be
               used at range using the skill Reach, while a Ranged(5 ft
               burst) would not work with the skill Reach, but would bene-
               fit from the passive Compound Eyes.

Accuracy Check

    Accuracy Check - A move labeled here with Attack, will
use your Attack Modifier and a d20 roll against a target's
Defense in order to hit. A move labeled here with Special
Attack, will use your Special Attack Modifier and a d20 roll
against a target's Special Defense in order to hit. A move
labeled here with Effect, will use your Speed Modifier and a
d20 roll against a target's Speed in order to hit.

                                                                                                       of order upon a specific trigger, including during other

                                                                                                       character's turns. When they do, they can only use that

                                                                                                       move on their turn- they can't move. Using a Reaction

                                                                                                       move interrupts the current turn until it completes.

Pokťmon Moves                          Hazards
                                          Whenever you create a hazard, you are usually centered

                                       in the middle of the hazard you create. Each hazard effect
                                       is defined per move. If a hazard move is used on the same
                                       area as an identical allied hazard, it immediately creates a
                                       second layer of that hazard that perfectly layers the first
                                       copy of that hazard. One can only be affected by a single
                                       hazard once per turn, but can still be affected by different
                                       kinds of hazards within the same turn.

                                                                 Intercepting Attacks

                                                                    When a trainer is hit by an attack, one of their Pokťmon
                                                                 can take its turn to move to their trainer and intercept the
                                                                 attack. This does not require the trainer's command for the
                                                                 round. The intercepting Pokťmon takes whatever damage
                                                                 and effects the attacker rolls for the successful attack.

Weather                                                             Intercepted attacks apply weaknesses and resistances,

Contest Revision contributions by Sol  Contests                                                         Contest Types

                                          In Pokťmon Tabletop Adventures, contests really shine            While each Pokťmon move has an elemental type for
                                       as a chance for players to get creative. Contests provide a      battle, they are all also aligned with a different Contest
                                       stage for your Pokťmon to stand out in ways other than           type. Additionally, Pokťmon who are attuned to any spe-
                                       battle. During a Contest, instead of trying to knock out your    cific stat will find that they are better suited to a specific

                                       opponents, you try to score points by impressing a panel of type of contest.

                                       Contest judges. Depending on the Contest and judge,              The contest types are Beauty, Clever, Cool, Cute, and

                                       different parts of your performance might matter different- Tough. These types compliment each other but sometimes
                                       ly- some judges might evaluate your Pokťmon's condition they clash.
                                       and grooming, while others only care about your actual

                                       performance. Either way, your goal is the same: impress                         Cool
                                       the judges to earn Star Points and win the contest.

                                           Star Points are your metric of victory. Each contest has a   Tough                   Beauty
                                       specific set of Star Points available to earn, each associated       Clever           Cute
                                       with a particular hidden goal set by the panel of judges. If
                                       you achieve that goal by the end of the contest, you get the
                                       point; the coordinator with the most Star Points wins. While
                                       these requirements for earning a Star Point are initially hid-
                                       den, researching the judges ahead of time can reveal their
                                       preferences. Usually, there are three judges, each with
                                       three Star Points to award.

                                          During the appeal stage of each contest, you will have a         In the diagram above, you can see which contest types
                                       chance to use your Pokťmon's moves to show off across            are complimentary and which types clash. When a contest
                                       five rounds of appeals. In each round, your appeal will earn     judge favors cute appeals, it's likely they'll be turned off by
                                       Heart Points, which are noted by the judges and taken into       any displays that are tough or cool. The same judge will
                                       account when they decide who to award Star Points to. If         probably not mind a beautiful or clever appeal on the other
                                       you haven't had the time to research the judges ahead of         hand.
                                       time, observing their reactions during your appeals can
                                       provide a clue to what they're looking for.

                                          Star Points can be awarded in a variety of ways. Most
                                       commonly, a point is awarded based on whoever earned
                                       the most Heart Points across all five rounds. However, it
                                       could be awarded to whomever earned the most Heart
                                       Points while using Clever moves, or Fire-type moves, or
                                       moves that are super-effective against Ice! There are doz-
                                       ens of ways to earn Star Points, and it's possible to earn
                                       Star Points from a judge who you were ignoring!

                                          In order to win, you must figure out how to manipulate
                                       your opponents, their Pokťmon and the Judges. At the be-
                                       ginning of each contest, at least one Contest Type will be
                                       announced. At least one judge will prefer that contest type.
                                       However, this will only determine your strategy for a single
                                       Star Point! You must still pay attention to the judges and
                                       guess what interests them. Sometimes they will be forth-
                                       right, other times they will be poker-faced. It's your job as a
                                       Pokťmon trainer to pick up on cues and master this form
                                       of Pokťmon competition if you're aiming to win coveted
                                       Contest Ribbons!

Original Additional Contest Rules by TheProfessor  Contest Variants                                                Double Performance

                                                      There's many variations of contests - and different ven-        In a Double Performance, each Coordinator uses two
                                                   ues may have different rules. Here are some variants you        Pokťmon, instead of one. For the Appeal Stage, both Pokť-
                                                   might encounter at a contest hall.                              mon participate together in each of their five appeals. Each
                                                                                                                   round, both Pokťmon select a move. These two moves
                                                                                                                   should are combined into one appeal that features both
                                                                                                                   moves. Neither Pokťmon can use a move they used in the
                                                                                                                   previous round, unless that move is Reliable. To determine
                                                                                                                   the combined move effects, select any combination of key-
                                                                                                                   word and contest type from the two moves. That combina-
                                                                                                                   tion determines the effects of the move.

                                                   Trainer-Appeal Contests                                            Example: A Pikachu that uses Thunder Wave (Cool, Ex-
                                                                                                                   citement) in combination with a Quagsire using Surf
                                                                                                                   (Beauty, Round Starter). As Quagsire raises the waters,
                                                                                                                   Pikachu uses its Thunder Wave to force the water into even
                                                                                                                   greater waves. Pikachu then shows off its cool surfing on
                                                                                                                   the back of Quagsire. The Coordinator decides to select the
                                                                                                                   Cool type from Thunder Wave, and the Round Starter key-
                                                                                                                   word from Surf, and rolls accordingly.

                                                      In Trainer Appeal Contests, it's not only the Pokťmon
                                                   that compete - the trainer's performance is just as crucial to
                                                   success as that of the Pokťmon! Heart Points may be
                                                   awarded for Trainers making appeals with speeches, musi-
                                                   cal performances, athletic feats, or even their own attack
                                                   moves!

                                                   Super Contests

                                                      While standard Contests have a fixed Contest Type, Su-
                                                   per Contests are completely random. At the beginning of
                                                   each round, before moves are performed, a contest type is
                                                   randomly chosen to matter. Often the known Star Point
                                                   category is "Highest total HP from appeals that match each
                                                   round's chosen contest type."

                                                   Festival

                                                   Festivals are much like Super Contests, in that the Con- Presentation Stage Options

                                                   test Type is constantly fluctuating. However, in a Festival,    Sometimes, judges will demand even more of a show

                                                   each judge announces a different desired contest type for past initial introductions and appeals. If they do, contests

                                                   appeals each round at random. The Star Point category           that engage in additional rounds and presentation stages

                                                   here "Highest to HP from appeals that match the requested will explicitly say so before hand unless the judges are real-

                                                   type during each round."                                        ly throwing contestants through the ringer and expecting

                                                   High Voltage                                                    them to perform on the spot with no plans! These variants
                                                                                                                   will absolutely award additional Star Points, so it's im-

                                                   In a High Voltage Contest, the Voltage rules are slightly portant to be flexible if you're going to win the Contest!

                                                   different. Each Judge begins with 1 Voltage. Whenever the       Fashion Show (Cute, Beauty) - Judges will also give

                                                   voltage of a judge is raised, the Pokťmon making the ap-
                                                   peal add Xd4 to their appeal where X is the judge's current themes and ask performers to match their wardrobes to

                                                   voltage level. Once a judge views everything as Electrifying,   als, brands, or any other details related to styles.

the Contest type. The judges of a contest also generally
will award a total of three Star Points each, but some are
wild cards who can even award bonus Star Points for
unexpected reasons. Here is a list of some things a
judge could be looking for in their criteria. This list is not
exhaustive! (HP is Heart Points)

Highest total HP from appeals

Second highest total HP from appeals

Highest Clever stat

Most appeals made to them specifically

Most HP from a first-stage Pokťmon

Most HP while using Normal-type Moves

Most damage done to a Steel dummy on Stage                             Contest Creativity
Most HP while using daily-frequency Moves
Most HP while using non-damaging moves                                    In Contests, you should take the chance to describe eve-
Most HP while using melee moves                                        ry appeal that you make with your Pokťmon. When you
Most HP while using 'kick' moves                                       train your Pokťmon you can rehearse their performances
Most HP while performing while in the air                              and when they reveal their appeals to judges on stage you
                                                                       can detail your Pokťmon's appeal. Appeals should only
                                                                       take about ten seconds, but whatever creativity you per-
                                                                       form can score additional d4 rolls during your final appeal
                                                                       awarded at your GM's discretion through the judges.

   During your introductory round, or during any round of
the appeals round, you may expend these stats to give           Appeal - 3d4
yourself more Heart Points. You can only use Contest Stats
if the move you are using matches the Contest Stat you          Appeal has no special effects.
want to expend. For example, if you are using Thunder
Shock, a Cool move, and you have a Cool Contest Stat of 6,      Attention Grabber - Special
you can add 6 points to your appeal to gain more Heart
Points for that appeal.                                         Attention Grabber looks at the Heart Points value of the
                                                                last move used (1d4, 2d4, 3d4) and uses the same value +1
   At the end of each contest, your Pokťmon's stats are         die.
restored. Once you give you Pokťmon their ten contest
stats, you can not change them anymore≠ they are perma-         Big Show - Special

                                                                Big Show rolls 8d4 if each contestant before you rolled at
                                                                least 3d4 for their appeal this round. You may only use the
                                                                effect of Big Show if you are performing last during the
                                                                final round of appeals.

                                                                Catching Up - 1d4

                                                                Catching Up rolls an additional +3d4 if you currently have
                                                                the lowest total Heart Points.

                                                                Crowd Pleaser - 1d4

                                                                Crowd Pleaser raises the target Judge's voltage by exactly 2,
                                                                regardless of the move's contest type.

                                                                Excitement - 2d4

                                                                Excitement prevents the target Judge's Voltage from going
                                                                down during that round of appeals.

                                                                Final Appeal - 2d4

                                                                Final Appeal rolls an additional 2d4 for your appeal if the
                                                                move is performed during the final round of appeals.

                                                                Get Ready! - None

                                                                Get Ready! Lets you roll twice the dice you normally would
                                                                for any reason during your next appeal.

                                                                Good Show! - 1d4

                                                                Good Show! rolls an additional +3d4 if any voltage was
                                                                raised during both of the last appeals during the same
                                                                round.

180
                                                                                      Seen Nothing Yet - 2d4
                                                                                      Seen Nothing Yet rolls an additional +2d4 when anyone has
                                                                                      raised a judge's voltage to max during the same round.
                                                                                      Special Attention - 1d4
                                                                                      Special Attention rolls an additional +3d4 if at least two ap-
                                                                                      peals were made before you and no other participant ap-
                                                                                      pealed to the same judge you are appealing to during that
                                                                                      round.
                                                                                      Torrential Appeal - Special
                                                                                      Torrential Appeal rolls +1d4 for each contestant who per-
                                                                                      formed before you this round.
                                                                                      Unsettling - 2d4
                                                                                      Unsettling lowers each judge's voltage 1 level.

Hold That Thought - 2d4
Hold That Thought prevents the target judge's voltage from
being raised for the remainder of the round, after your ap-
peal.
Incredible - 1d4
Incredible raises each judge's voltage 1 level, regardless of
contest types.
Incentives - 2d4
Incentives rolls an additional +2d4 when their appeal raises
the judge's voltage.
Inversed Appeal - Special
Inversed Appeal rolls 4d4, reduced by 1d4 for every point
of the target judge's Voltage
Reflective Appeal - Special
Reflective Appeal rolls 1d4 for each point of voltage the
target judge has, up to 4d4.
Reliable - 2d4
Reliable may use the same move during your next round
and still gain appeal points. When you do, you also roll an
additional +1d4 during the appeal.
Round Ender - 2d4
Round Ender rolls an additional +2d4 during your turn if
you make your appeal last that round.
Round Starter - 2d4
Round Starter rolls an additional +2d4 during your turn if
you make your appeal first that round.
Scrambler - 2d4
Scrambler randomizes the order of appeals during the next
round. Appeals must be made in that order.

                   The Pokťmon World                                              Pokť Marts

                   In the Pokťmon world, there are many places that you           Pokť Marts are often found in the corner of a Pokťmon

                   can count on finding. As an adventuring trainer, you should Center. Many Pokť Marts found in Pokťmon Centers have a
                   know about these locales in case you'll need help at a Pokť- pretty limited selection focused on medical items for travel,

                   mon Center or if you need to pick up supplies at a Pokť        but Pokť Marts that have their own storefronts have a

                   Mart.                                                          great selection of items to use in the Pokťmon World.

                   Pokťmon Centers

                      Pokťmon Centers exist to heal Pokťmon. They have in-
                   credible technology that can restore a Pokťmon's health
                   and cure it of all afflictions while the Pokťmon are still in
                   their Pokť Balls. Many regions in the world don't even
                   charge a fee to restore Pokťmon to health!

                                                                                     Many Pokť Marts also double as supermarkets that have
                                                                                  food and goods for your home, or on the go. These larger
                                                                                  Pokť Marts also will have kits and other trainer pack bun-
                                                                                  dles. Larger department stores will have even more op-
                                                                                  tions, from clothes, to tools, or even various tech you might
                                                                                  need on the road, or just in your home office.

                      Pokťmon Centers are also equipped to heal Pokťmon
                   who are not in their Poke Balls. Doctors and nurses will
                   work as long as it takes to heal your Pokťmon, though it
                   only takes a couple of minutes if the Pokťmon is within its
                   Pokť Balls. In larger cities, Pokťmon Centers also double as
                   hospitals. In smaller towns, Pokťmon Centers are usually
                   sperate from any medical centers that would work on heal-
                   ing humans.

                      Like Pokťmon centers, many hospitals will also work to
                   heal those who need it. Some regions do have bills, howev-
                   er.

The Pokťmon World     Pokťmon Centers will never charge more than 100 P to
                   fully restore your whole Pokťmon team, and most do it for
                   free, but hospital visits might cost something for a trainer,
                   though most regions in the world have free health care.

                                                                                  A Pokťmon Center with a Pokť Mart inside.

                   Battle Chateau

                      The Battle Chateau prides itself as being a classy form of
                   the Pokťmon league with members holding noble titles
                   such as Baron, Countess, Marchioness, or Duke. They ex-
                   pect a certain dťcor and poise of their trainers, above the
                   shouting rabble of the Pokťmon League.

                                                                                  Battle Subways

                                                                                     Battle Subways are elaborate tunnels of battle after bat-
                                                                                  tle that often lead to battles against their station masters.
                                                                                  In such limited spaces, trainers need to find creative ways
                                                                                  to win.

                   Battle Frontier

                      Appearing as a sort of amusement park, the Battle Fron-     Battle Towers
                   tier is a place of wonderous and out-of-norm competition.
                   Frontier Brains concoct strange rules for battle, pushing
                   trainers to solve near-impossible odds in battle and still
                   come out on top. While Frontiers found everywhere wel-
                   come challengers of all kinds, Frontier Brains tend to hate
                   having their time wasted by overzealous trainers. Come
                   prepared for a challenge.

                                                                                     Climbing high into the sky by battling floor after floor is
                                                                                  the goal of any region's Battle Tower. They don't often
                                                                                  have special rules, but they are almost never associated
                                                                                  with the region's Pokťmon League. Instead, they act as a
                                                                                  private means of testing your skill as a trainer without the
                                                                                  glitz and glamour of a stadium.

The Pokťmon World  206
Ranger Stations                                                  Game Corners

   The Ranger Union has stations located throughout the             Gambling halls for games of chance and skill are found

   Depending on their captain or regional law, rangers              It could just be pointless rumors and nonsense but

   While rangers do care about people being in danger,
they are primarily tasked with protecting the Pokťmon
world and its balance of human and Pokťmon coexisting.
For this reason, sometimes it's better to just tell a police
officer. Luckily, police stations are often connected directly
to ranger stations in larger cities since the two organiza-
tions often work together to protect everyone that they
can.

                   Grooming Parlors and Pokťmon Salons

                      Different Pokťmon parlors and salons can give your
                   Pokťmon a spa day of rest and cleaning. Pokťmon with fur
                   can be shaped and Pokťmon with stone carapaces or
                   blades can be polished. Entire buildings can potentially be
                   many different groomer's store fronts for various types of
                   Pokťmon.

                                                                                   Routes help to connect human settlements, but not all
                                                                                settlements are connected by real ranger-made routes.
                                                                                Sometimes what becomes a route is just trailblazes made
                                                                                by hundreds of trainers who have passed through before.

The Pokťmon World     Pokťmon love being pampered, but it's important that
                   you're not leaving an ill-tempered or Pokťmon who is too
                   young with a stranger. That might make what's supposed
                   to be a relaxing experience into a terrible one for every-
                   one, including the groomers you left your Pokťmon with.

                   208
Transportation                                                    Luxury cruise ships also serve as a way to travel be-
                                                               tween ports, even from region to region. While the ships

While different regions may rely on different means of are not aiming for speed, often traveling for a week instead

transporting people from town to town, or city to city, there of a plane ride's quick flight, it is not an uncommon journey

are several common means that are often found through- as there is often sight-seeing, Pokťmon battling on deck,,

out the world.                                                 or other fun Pokťmon competitions. Some regions even

                                                               feature Battle Facilities on deck, or may sport a Pokťmon

                                                               Gym, run by the ship's captain.

In heavily developed regions where cities are connected

by suburbs, neighborhoods, and shopping centers with

grocery markets, it's not uncommon for a simple bus ride

or underground rail system to exist between two points of

interest.

   At the same time, within large cities, cabs or other pri-      In some regions, many have turned to Pokťmon-
vate driving services may be able to take you from the city's  powered transportation. In Galar, many rely on the region's
outer Pokťmon Center to its central district where a Pokť-     Corviknight cabs to cross wild, overrun areas to get from
mon Gym facility may be located.                               city to city. Other regions such as Alola rent out Pokťmon
                                                               to ferry people between islands.

   Not everyone is a trainer, nor does everyone have a                                                                                                            The Pokťmon World
Pokťmon capable of transoceanic travel by sea or sky, so
airports are common in developed regions. Not every re-
gion has airports in every major town, but for prices rang-
ing from 200 P for two-hour flights that cross seas or
mountains or 1000 P flights that cross oceans or conti-
nents, it's not uncommon for trainers to find a time-
efficient flight to whatever place they need to be to contin-
ue their adventure.

Combat Demo  226
Combat Demo Notes                                                                                                                                                 Combat Demo

   Not all battles in gym may be as short or smooth, espe-
cially once you consider that some gym leaders will want to
battle with and against more than one Pokťmon from a
trainer's roster. Wild battles similarly may not end in weak-
er Pokťmon fleeing, instead its possible that knocking out a
family's leader may inspire more aggression from other
wild Pokťmon.

   While the Sunny Day served to protect Tsareena from
afflictions, if any Pokťmon began to use Fire-attacks or Wa-
ter-type attacks they would be affected. Keep track of
things that may affect your Pokťmon's moves and similar
effects that may change how an attack works or how move-
ment is changed.

   This GM did not change any of the NPC Pokťmon's stats
with natures. This is totally okay, as natures rarely matter
for GM-controlled Pokťmon and can always be rolled if a
trainer captures a Pokťmon. However, sometimes adding a
nature to a gym leader's Pokťmon can give them an ad-
vantage in battle or a specific nature could justify a wild
Pokťmon's aggressive behavior.

   Some readers may have noticed that Mallow's Tsareena
had 13 Defense, when the species naturally has only 11. It's
perfectly normal for a GM to change stat passives around
on NPC Pokťmon or give them vitamins to raise stats and
give players a small amount of additional difficulty during
an important battle.

     Player and Pokťmon Tokens created and used in these combat demos were made at::
            https://rolladvantage.com/tokenstamp
                                                                                                                                                             227
             Contest Demo

                In the following example contest demonstration, players
             Elle, Kay, and Ray will be competing against each other and
             two NPCs to earn a Tough Ribbon at a Contest Hall. This is
             just an example, as Contest Halls can have complex criteria
             for judging and may even have more than three judges at a
             time. Some of the following Trainer profiles will be missing
             features. This is because not all features are relevant to
             Contests.

                                          Kay is an Ace Trainer. She's a bold risk
                                      taker who will occasionally overextend to
                                      try and accomplish short term goals in
                                      order to achieve victory. She's preforming
                                      with her first time contestant Machoke.

             Kay Linh - Level 2          Ace Trainer, Gym Trainer Origin                          Ray is a Breeder. They are a nurturing
                                                                                               explorer who is hoping to grow their
             Hit Points: 20 Attack: 6 (+3)     Defense: 3 (+1)                                 Pokťmon family during their adventure.
                                                                                               Today they are trying to earn their first
             Speed: 4 (+2) Sp. Attack: 5 (+2)  Sp. Defense: 2 (+1)                             ribbon with their Gastrodon.

             Features:

             Strategy Insider (Commanding, Nurturing) - Intuitive                   Ray Shell - Level 2  Breeder, Backpacker Origin
             knowledge about league trainers

                                                                                                   Hit Points: 20 Attack: 3 (+1)  Defense: 6 (+3)
             Improved Attacks - Pokťmon get +3 damage with Attack, +2

             damage with Special Attack                                             Speed: 4 (+2) Sp. Attack: 2 (+1)              Sp. Defense: 5 (+2)

                        Machoke: Bold                                               Features:

                        Fighting - Medium, Heavy                                    Natural Edge - Your Pokťmon hatch with a permanent +1
                        Skills - Strength                                           to any stat

                                                                      In contests, contestants perform when they want to. If
                                                                   there is a dispute, then you default to the Pokťmon's speed
                                                                   to see who performs first in the round.

                                                                   Sharing Contest Knowledge

*** Some example trainers are all using evolved Pokťmon               Kay, Ray, and Elle freely share their knowledge with each     Combat Demo
who are each at least at loyalty 3. While this is possible in any  other because they don't care who gets first and second as
campaign by level 2, it's not a standard to compare yourself to    long as at least one of them gets to earn an honor. Some-
and are written this way for the purposes of this example con-     times, competitive party members won't share their
test. In this scenario, the GM have given each player a level for  knowledge of Star Point categories. If players are not coop-
their first major achievement≠ Kay and Ray leveled up upon         erating here, it shouldn't cause problems as long as every-
earning their first badge while Elle leveled up upon receiving     one is in on the competitive-minded contest. If players
their first ribbon.                                                aren't on the same page, it can cause problems later if

   Unless the GM says otherwise, dummies can't have status
afflictions and you don't need to roll accuracy checks when
attacking them. However, sometimes tougher dummies or
moving dummies will need accuracy checks. Will-O-Wisp is a

since she knows that Fire-type moves matter for this contest.

                                                                                                                                                                    239
                                         At its core, Pokťmon Tabletop Adventures is about realizing your Pokť-
                                     mon adventures that aren't found in other Pokťmon media. Part of this is
                                     making sure everyone's having fun. Tabletop offers an unlimited amount of
                                     experiences and scenarios, but that doesn't always mean any player will
                                     enjoy any given scenario.

                                         Game Masters: Always make sure players know what kind of things to
                                     expect in a campaign in terms of any less friendly themes and do a good
                                     job of stopping poor player behavior that's directly affecting other players'
                                     ability to enjoy gameplay.

                                         Players: Know what kind of game you're getting into, try to make sure
                                     you're not stepping over any lines, and be aware of whether or not you're
                                     making yourself the center of attention. There are other players, including
                                     the GM, so it's important to give everyone a voice and not cut off other
                                     players with too many interruptions.

                                         Whenever there's any problem talk it out. Together, tabletop games can
                                     stay fun and keep going. When players and game masters only treat each
                                     other as opponents, sidekicks, or obstacles instead of treating each other
                                     as people, games fall apart and end.
